title: Explaining the Recent explain-html-gen Diff
summary: This walkthrough explains three related changes: safer default output paths for snap-confined browsers, randomized quiz answer ordering, and a cross-target build script for Linux and Windows on AMD64/ARM64.
author: Local diff analysis

background.intro: The generator takes structured JSON and emits a single self-contained HTML page with interactive quiz behavior. The CLI decides where to write that page, the builder composes markup and script data, and a validator checks structural constraints.
background.mental_model: Think of the toolchain as three layers: input schema -> HTML builder -> runtime quiz interaction. The diff modifies each layer's reliability boundaries rather than adding a new feature area.

components.name: CLI output path resolver
components.description: Chooses default destination when --output is omitted and creates missing directories.
components.role: Ensures generated files are reachable by local tooling and browsers

components.name: Quiz card formatter
components.description: Builds each quiz question card, option list, and embedded correct-index metadata consumed by JavaScript.
components.role: Controls both visible option order and correctness mapping

components.name: Cross-target build script
components.description: Compiles binaries for Linux and Windows across amd64 and arm64 architectures.
components.role: Produces distributable binaries from one command

background.prior: Previously, default output went to /tmp, which can fail for snap-confined Firefox file access. Quiz options were rendered in source order, making correctness position predictable from input if not randomized elsewhere. Multi-arch builds required manual one-off commands.

intuition.intro: The unifying idea is practical robustness: make the generated artifact easier to open, make quiz outcomes harder to guess by position, and make binaries easier to ship.
intuition.core_idea: Move defaults toward user-accessible locations, add explicit option shuffling with correct-index remapping, and codify cross-compilation in one repeatable script.
intuition.old_behavior: Default path was fixed at /tmp, quiz option order matched input ordering, and platform builds were ad-hoc.
intuition.new_behavior: Default path now prefers ~/Downloads then home, quiz options are shuffled via Fisher-Yates and correctness is remapped, and one script emits Linux/Windows artifacts for amd64 and arm64.
intuition.trade_offs_edge_cases: Randomization may still occasionally look uneven for only five questions, so validator warnings can appear heuristically. Also, output path selection now depends on home-directory availability and Downloads existence.

code.intro: The diff clusters into three execution paths: path resolution in CLI runtime, question rendering in builder logic, and release artifact generation in shell scripting.

subsections.title: Default Output Path Migration
subsections.explanation: The CLI now builds a filename once and chooses a destination by accessibility priority: Downloads, then home, then /tmp fallback.

blocks.caption: The default destination changes from a hardcoded /tmp to a browser-friendly preference chain.

subsections.consequences: Users can usually open generated files directly in Firefox snap without moving files manually, while still preserving a deterministic fallback.

subsections.title: Quiz Option Randomization With Correct Remapping
subsections.explanation: The builder creates one RNG per page build, shuffles each question's options, and stores the remapped correct index in data attributes.

blocks.caption: A runtime seed means repeated generations do not reuse the same question-order mapping.
Correctness stays semantically stable even though display order changes.

subsections.consequences: Readers can no longer infer correctness from static option position in generated markup; quiz feedback remains accurate because the index is remapped.

subsections.title: Codified Multi-Arch Build Outputs
subsections.explanation: A shell script now compiles four targets in one run: Linux and Windows for amd64 and arm64.

blocks.code:
echo "Building Linux executables..."

for arch in amd64 arm64; do
  output="$OUT_DIR/${APP_NAME}-linux-${arch}"
  GOOS=linux GOARCH="$arch" CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o "$output" "$ENTRYPOINT"
done

echo "Building Windows executables..."

for arch in amd64 arm64; do
  output="$OUT_DIR/${APP_NAME}-windows-${arch}.exe"
  GOOS=windows GOARCH="$arch" CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o "$output" "$ENTRYPOINT"
done
blocks.caption: Target matrix is explicit and reproducible from a single script invocation.

subsections.consequences: Release prep becomes less error-prone by eliminating manual architecture flag repetition.

quiz.question: Why did the default output location change away from always using /tmp?
options: To reduce generated file size by using a faster filesystem path
To avoid write permissions that Go lacks when running without sudo
To improve accessibility for snap-confined browsers that may not read /tmp
To guarantee that all files are automatically deleted after browser close
quiz.explanation: The change targets browser accessibility, especially snap-confined Firefox environments where /tmp access can fail even when file generation succeeded.

quiz.question: What makes the new quiz randomization preserve correctness after shuffling?
options: It stores only the original index and compares against an unshuffled copy at click time
It tracks each option's original index during shuffle and computes a new correct index
It keeps correct answers in position two and shuffles only distractors for consistency
It relies on JavaScript to discover the right answer from explanation text heuristics
quiz.explanation: The shuffle logic pairs option text with original indices, then determines the remapped position where the original correct option landed.

quiz.question: What should you infer if the validator still warns about answer-position balance on some runs?
options: Randomization is definitely broken because any warning proves deterministic ordering
The generator now validates wrong because data-correct-idx is no longer emitted
The warning is heuristic and can trigger with only five samples even when shuffling works
The quiz file is malformed and must contain exactly six questions for valid distribution
quiz.explanation: Balance checks over five questions are small-sample heuristics. A skew in one generated page does not imply shuffle failure.

quiz.question: Why does the build script set CGO_ENABLED=0 across all target builds?
options: It avoids cross-compilation failures caused by host C toolchain dependencies
It enables faster HTML rendering by disabling JavaScript in generated pages
It ensures Windows binaries are packaged with GTK modules automatically
It forces static routing tables to be embedded at compile time in the quiz logic
quiz.explanation: Disabling CGO keeps cross-builds portable because targets do not depend on host-specific C compilers or runtime libraries.

quiz.question: Which path selection behavior is implemented when --output is omitted?
options: Use /tmp first, then Downloads, then current working directory
Use home directory first, then /tmp, and never attempt Downloads
Use Downloads if it exists under home, else home, else /tmp
Use the repository root to keep generated pages version-controlled by default
quiz.explanation: The code checks for a home directory, prefers home/Downloads when present, otherwise falls back to home and finally /tmp if home is unavailable.
